textByHashes.fd4d2dfb20016b421059185648a7c3820f4150e9: Hello there. Would you like to exchange research?
textByHashes.43462772ea6b285c01ef3a9077e8bccc834fd6ad: Very good! I'll happily exchange some treasures I've found in return for your findings!
textByHashes.18eb5cbf20a25ee3313945e1b4c0996c1750d853: Are you sure? It looks like you already have one.
textByHashes.899b310464928ade704325026825324c9fb982ae: Not a problem! Here you go.
textByHashes.75b1351de2fddce390d894ddeec6db7f8222cce4: Well, I'll be here if you need anything.
textByHashes.fa64e2cfe2a8ecf3be68bdcf985a606151cfe2a5: Grand work! You'll want this as a reward!
textByHashes.fa9085fa0f37ad73b12e2a15ff33cd5ca9cd55dc: Oh, sorry! I was lost in thought. You gave me quite the start!
textByHashes.358ed4a161945eb70f466dc55f9c5732ee0f07f6: Yes! It's these ents you see! I'd never seen anything like them until I came here. Look, here comes one now...
textByHashes.ea3ff2b24293a83afd22fa2b2db203dcea554efe: Exactly! These totems are dotted all around here. They seem to be very important to the ents, but I don't know why.
textByHashes.64b73f2d819f46ac8e45dc0e4427846819c1210c: Yes, but the locals didn't really give me much to go on. All they told me is that the ents, totems and spirits are all part of the natural cycle here in the valley.
textByHashes.6d4a5e1ee3776bfa112ddf88efdcde40fb7b8a4a: Good idea. If nothing else, it will be useful to document the carvings on it. Come, let's have a look...
textByHashes.41e056f60584939d816179bc71d862c6c6261a2d: Well, actually my name is Ranulph, not funny man...
textByHashes.b65834d75ab8e18468dff9ec67ee646ddba4f6e1: Hmm... I think the child's assessment is correct. The ents probably won't be happy about what we've done.
textByHashes.a7c54a9214c8fd9f6dd5ac4e02b39ca3dfc11478: Let's see... First, we'll need some lumber to rebuild the pole. I reckon one set of logs will do it.
textByHashes.69e070f7a81a1f118c0b6414e1d3fcda5bfbbc65: It looks like it was made from oak, so presumably oak logs should be enough. However, I do wonder if using better wood would help it survive the elements better...
textByHashes.7270f961b9508a95882f7447caf5f1b978813b5a: Hmm... The pole is only going to be the beginning though. We're going to need to carve it... I really should have made more notes on the carvings...
textByHashes.465e6760a577b3c1e446804b329ee53c47087ead: Did you see what that ent did?
textByHashes.1b3d8f4b52486aa20a5ce33125bdf0560c03cf2b: Any luck getting that totem rebuilt?
textByHashes.c660f4e26726f20cffb3f46ae77c8261d3a138e9: I reckon you'll just need one set of logs. Oak logs should be enough, but I do wonder if using better wood would help it survive the elements better...
textByHashes.697e9a3507fbe49c7d241243730cee5004935e04: Yes, according to Isadora, the carvings that were on the totem represented the animal spirits around it. However, the animals were disturbed when the totem fell. Some left, but new ones arrived.
textByHashes.fe721709c8ce9eff141e5dfd2ae0156e26a39509: The right ones being the animals that are currently in this area.
textByHashes.9e8ae6553f35cf7807a0de3938e3f021f683e2b4: Don't forget, you'll need to make carvings of the animal spirits that are currently in this area.
textByHashes.33c1374efd746d9e568b1517c9fc7068d323f8fb: Remember, you need to make carvings of the animal spirits that are currently in this area.
textByHashes.6de438213f94876be3e306b8b67d5273f209ef84: You've quite the hand at carving, my friend.
textByHashes.17f34ffb4461958cc9115288d58d96db2260a98d: Now, the finishing touches. Apparently, the ents appreciate it when wooden items are returned to the forest.
textByHashes.4c0c70a8024310f2be019525dada1e73079a84f4: The cycle of life, death and decay, I suppose.
textByHashes.9152ec0fc990fedb32ea83d1311cd4c0b44b5f81: Exactly! Try decorating the totem with those kinds of items. I reckon four should do. Oh, and be sure to use the same wood you used for the totem itself.
textByHashes.338e9f3c73526abfcff6146257f5ac03d365d78f: How's the decorating of that totem going?
textByHashes.25d2228c09c1875286c15f045e84d74299eaab34: What Isadora said basically. I reckon four should do. Oh, and be sure to use the same wood you used for the totem itself.
textByHashes.bd90e00203c4525ebc39feb54a9b51e3ded9af5e: Look at that! I think it looks even better than it did before!
textByHashes.ad819dca6ce79d677fc48119a0d041f1133aa1fa: I couldn't have imagined a better outcome! Not only have the ents accepted our new totem, but they've also left something behind!
textByHashes.09e15bf930aed7c1355848e91011b58cecfadc9f: Well, this is definitely the most 'dialogue' I've had with the ents. Isadora here has been a great help as well!
textByHashes.eb5214199fb4baec0f9a0c2ec7ad276df02e1562: It seems the ents are very appreciative of those who maintain their totems. Given you seem to have a knack for it, you might want to keep helping them. They have plenty of other totem sites in the valley.
textByHashes.aa227564afb849bbb714012b6a9aa0dcba3db600: As for me, I'm going to keep studying them. I've learnt so much today, both from Isadora and from the ents themselves. I've already documented it all, but I'm sure there's more to discover!
textByHashes.2227d7e24295abbdb1f81a89a3f3bcd7f1b8d3e0: Speaking of which, here, take a notebook for yourself. I've left a copy of my notes in it, but you can also use it to document your own findings.
textByHashes.fe13f793e70fecef1ac1a0e15715f4cdf13fee9e: Be sure to check in once in a while to compare findings. I'll happily trade items I find around the valley in exchange for your research assistance!
